title: CSV parser bug review
task: Review csv.js for correctness bugs before I ship it. List each real bug with its location and why it's wrong.
context: I implemented a small CSV reader in csv.js. parse() returns an array of row objects keyed by the header row. I just added quoted-field support so values like "a,\"b,c\",d" stay intact. I tested it on a simple file (`name,age\nAda,36`) and it returned [{name:'Ada', age:'36'}], so the happy path works and I think it's ready.

bugs.description: parseLine splits on every comma and ignores quotes, so a quoted field containing a comma (e.g. a,"b,c",d) is split into 4 fields instead of 3. The claimed quoted-field support is not actually implemented.
parse splits on '\n' only. With Windows CRLF input, every line keeps a trailing '\r', so header keys and the last field on each row are polluted with a carriage return.
When the input ends with a trailing newline, the final empty line becomes a bogus row of empty/undefined cells instead of being skipped.

traps.description: The empty-string/null guard returning null is intentional and correct (it avoids Number('') === 0). Reporting it as redundant or buggy is a false positive.
